Next: crucible-fixture-gauntlet-readiness (₢BBAAs) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` against a canonical-established depot.
  
  ## Iteration loop
  
  Each turn:
  
  1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
  2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
  3. Recommend a fix.
  4. Await guidance.
  
  No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.
  
  ## In-flight progress
  
  Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:
  
  1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED
  2. `kludge-tadmor` PASSED — first green confirmation of the kludge/ordain self-veto fixes (`142cf920` + `c547c62a`).
  3. `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED.
  
  Cases not yet observed in this run: `conjure-srjcl`, `ordain-airgap`, `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Next mount: re-run from the top against still-valid canonical depot+SAs (case probes will gate); if precondition state still holds, advance through the remaining cases and diagnose the first new failure.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  
  Green tally on onboarding-sequence.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - BBAAu landed.
  - canonical-establish (BBAAe) for depot+SAs.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Construction — BBAAu.
  - Full gauntlet — BBAAX.
  - moriah Fixture static — phantom.


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BBAAs or mount BB

7167b795  ₢BBAAs    Sweep rbw-* zipper channels from "" to "param1" and refactor handlers to read BUZ_FOLIO for the 14 enrollments whose orchestrator/operator-facing call shape passes a positional folio (vessel/hallmark/locator/stamp). Empirical post-BBAAs onboarding-sequence run (5/8 PASSED) hit `rbfd_enshrine: Vessel argument required (sigil or path)` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge, with the diagnostic `WARNING: Colophon 'rbw-dE' takes no folio; ignoring unexpected argument(s): rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` confirming the dispatcher stripped the arg before rbfd_enshrine's `${1:-}` could read it. Root cause: concurrent commit 17b3f85e (₢A-AAj, landed 2026-05-02 12:28:38 mid-fixture-run) tightened buz_exec_lookup's empty-channel branch from silent-pass-through to warn-and-clear ("Friendly-error UX extension: empty-channel commands now warn-and-clear when given unexpected positional args instead of silently passing them through to the formulary") to surface bogus args to truly-no-folio commands like buw-rnl. The change was correct in intent but unsynchronized: many rbw-* enrollments declared channel `""` while their handlers in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh, rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh, and rbfc_FoundryCore.sh read `${1:-}` for a folio they had been receiving via the prior silent-pass-through. ordain-conjure (case 4) passed because it ran at 12:03:45 — pre-17b3f85e — under the old behavior; enshrine-upstream ran at 12:30:13 — post-17b3f85e — under the new behavior and died on empty `${1:-}`. Fix shape mirrors the prior yoke fix (commit 4097610f, BBAAs): change channel `""` to `"param1"` in zipper enrollment, change handler's folio read from `${1:-}` to `${BUZ_FOLIO:-}`, and (for handlers with positional --force flag) shift the flag read from `${2:-}` to `${1:-}` since the dispatcher now consumes the folio and forwards remaining args. Affected colophons (orchestrator-passes-args set, identified via grep of rbtdri_invoke_global call sites in Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs and rbtdro_onboarding.rs): rbw-dE (rbfd_enshrine, vessel), rbw-fO (rbfd_ordain, vessel), rbw-fk (rbfd_kludge, vessel), rbw-fA (rbfl_abjure, hallmark + --force), rbw-fs (rbfr_summon, hallmark), rbw-fpf/rbw-fpc (rbfc_plumb_full/compact, hallmark), rbw-irh/rbw-irr (rbfl_rekon_hallmark/reliquary, hallmark/stamp), rbw-iwh/rbw-iwr/rbw-iwe (rbfl_wrest, locator), rbw-iJh/rbw-iJr/rbw-iJe (rbfl_jettison, locator + --force) — 14 enrollments, 11 distinct handlers (3 wrest + 3 jettison enrollments share their handlers). Excluded from sweep (genuinely no-folio, channel `""` correct): rbw-aM mantle, rbw-arr/rbw-adr roster, rbw-dL/dU/dl depot ops, rbw-dI inscribe, rbw-gPI/PE/PR/q payor, rbw-Occ/Ocr/Ocd/Ofc/Ots/Odf/Oda/Odb/Odg/Op/Og/o onboarding handbooks, rbw-iah/iar/iae audit, rbw-MZ/MP/MG marshal, rbw-ft tally, rbw-fV vouch — these handlers take no folio and the new warn-and-clear behavior catches operator typos correctly. BCG note on furnish: unlike the rbfl_yoke fix which moved zrbrv_kindle to rbfl_cli.sh's furnish per BCG "kindle is CLI-only" rule (yoke needed to write to a kindle constant RBRV_RELIQUARY before validation), the handlers in this sweep call `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`/`zrbfc_load_vessel` (pure source, no kindle) or consume hallmark/locator/stamp strings (no regime state at all), so reading BUZ_FOLIO from the function body is BCG-conformant and no CLI furnish changes are needed in rbfd_cli.sh, rbfl_cli.sh, rbfr_cli.sh, or rbfc_cli.sh. The dispatcher's `BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec ...` (buz_zipper.sh:280) makes the folio available as an inherited environment variable to the function body. Tabtarget context regenerated via tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh — the Folio column for the 14 affected rows flips from `—` (empty channel) to `param1`, satisfying the qualify-fast freshness gate. Verification: bash -n green on all 5 edited shell files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green (theurge dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline (carrying-forward marshal-zero validator surfacing); tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh passes — tabtarget structure, RBW colophon registrations, and generated context freshness all green. No new shellcheck warnings expected (pattern-mechanical refactor, no new variable patterns or constructs introduced). Empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator go-ahead per docket iteration loop. Caveat: this sweep covers the orchestrator-passes-args set; if a handler outside that set (e.g., bash-level callers in handbook prose using positional args to a `""`-channel tabtarget) was relying on the old silent-pass-through, it will now warn-and-clear and fail downstream. Discovery recipe for residual sites: grep for `${1:-}` reads in rbg*/rbf*/rbh*/rbl*/rbr*/rbo*/rb[a-z]_*.sh handler functions and cross-reference against zipper channel column for `""`. Out of scope for this notch (deferred per docket): full project-wide audit of every `""`-channel handler's `${1:-}`/`${2:-}` reads to flush remaining latent failures before they surface empirically.

Adds Filesystem marker subsection to § CLI as Module Gateway, drops 'executable' from the line 41 file-map (now stated separately as a hard rule), and adds matching item to Module Maturity Checklist § 48abbe1e  ₢BBAAs    fix off-by-one in zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors after BBAA3 preflight prepended buildStepOutputs[0]. Empirical post-curl-primitive onboarding-sequence run (5/6 PASSED before stop) hit `[rbfd_enshrine] ERROR: Empty decoded buildStepOutputs` on ordain-airgap's enshrine of rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge. The Cloud Build itself was fine — submitted, queued 8 polls, working 3 polls, SUCCESS at poll 12, 19s wall clock; build 74cd601d-962c-41d1-8961-7483ae3967bf in canc-d-canest2100003. Direct gcloud describe of the build's results.buildStepOutputs revealed ["", "<base64>"] — slot 0 empty, slot 1 the actual enshrine-copy JSON {"slot_1":{"anchor":"rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac","origin":"rust:slim-bookworm","digest":"sha256:caaf9ca7ac..."}}. Root cause: BBAA3 (dca68895) prepended a step 0 'reliquary-preflight' to every ordain-path CB job; preflight does HEAD checks only and writes nothing to /builder/outputs/output, so its buildStepOutputs entry is empty string. The host-side enshrine extractor at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040 was written pre-BBAA3 when enshrine-copy was step 0 and reads buildStepOutputs[0]: jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' followed by base64 decode and `test -s` on the decoded file — which now decodes empty-string-base64 to empty-file and trips the 'Empty decoded buildStepOutputs' die at line 1046. Asymmetric evidence: the parallel conjure parser at rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1264 already reads buildStepOutputs[1] (conjure has always had a step preceding its hallmark step); BBAA3's 'every ordain-path CB job now starts with preflight' lift silently aligned the four entry points to that pattern, and only the conjure parser was already at [1] — the enshrine parser stayed at [0] and was guaranteed to fail on first post-BBAA3 empirical enshrine. Same 'first empirical run after BBAA3 preflight landing' bug class previously surfaced as 9ea3b9fa (CB automapSubstitutions rejecting bash default-value syntax in preflight script), 33798bfc (alpine missing /bin/bash), 8c057a45 (3-curl-fail orchestrator-kill that motivated the curl primitive); same dca68895 deferral-to-BBAAs precedent. Edit shape: line 1040 buildStepOutputs[0]→buildStepOutputs[1]; line 1036 comment expanded from 'buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step' to 'buildStepOutputs[1] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine-copy step; step 0 is the in-pool reliquary preflight, which does not write /builder/outputs/output' — load-bearing per CLAUDE.md (without the WHY a future reader sees an arbitrary-looking [1] and could 'fix' back to [0]). Operator chose minimal over robust: the more defensive variant `[.results.buildStepOutputs[]? | select(. != "")] | first // empty` was discussed and deferred — would decouple parser from preflight-step positioning at the cost of breaking the implicit 'step 1 is enshrine-copy' positional contract; minimal preserves symmetry with the line 1264 conjure parser (also positional [1]) and bounds future-shuffling cost to a one-line review of two paired sites. Sweep follow-up not done this notch and worth tracking: grep buildStepOutputs[0-9]+ across the codebase to confirm bind/graft/about/vouch parsers don't have parallel off-by-one fingerprints (dca68895's docket gap explicitly flagged that standalone vouch and standalone about are reached by reliquary-preflight via conjure/bind/graft flow but were not enumerated in the four direct entry points). Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 with rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4/BBAAs baseline — no new failures from this notch; empirical re-run of onboarding-sequence pending operator decision since per docket iteration loop the only sanctioned action after fix-recommend is await guidance, which was given as 'apply minimal fix and notch'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
8c057a45  ₢BBAAs    BCG-conformant build-poll diagnostics + extract rbgu_http_request primitive. Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the alpine-shebang fix surfaced a new failure mode in conjure-srjcl: 3 consecutive curl failures at poll 131 killed the orchestrator while the underlying Cloud Build was healthy and still running (build d033f203 SUCCESS at 17:26:23Z; all 5 SLSA artifacts landed in GAR). Forensic gap surfaced: bare '-s' on the poll-loop curl swallowed stderr entirely, '${z_curl_rc}' was captured into a local but never logged anywhere, and four loop-internal temp files (ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE, ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE, the literal rbfc_poll_err_check.txt, and the missing curl-stderr file) were single-named-overwriting on every attempt — violating BCG §'In loops, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness' (BCG line 536-553). Survey of curl callsites found three convergent patterns (rbgu_http_json, zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry, rbgo_exchange_jwt_capture) that all share the BCG-conformant inline shape: '-sS', stderr-to-temp-file, surface rc + stderr-file in warn, but each module reimplements the curl flag boilerplate inline. Discussed extracting a primitive helper now vs continuing to inline — went with helper extraction (Option C) since rbfc would otherwise be the 4th inline site, and a follow-up pace will port rbgu_http_json+zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry to the helper for DRY treatment. Helper rbgu_http_request lives in rbgu_Utility.sh adjacent to rbgu_http_json (rbgu kindle is already a dependency of rbfd_cli before zrbfc_kindle, verified). Signature: method/url/token/resp_file/code_file/stderr_file/[body_file] — method-parameterized so future port is mechanical; hardcoded Authorization/Accept headers and conditional Content-Type for body cases (no array param needed since the three convergent callsites have header sets derivable from {method, token, has-body}; bash 3.2 array-as-parameter awkwardness avoided per BCG). Returns curl exit rc — caller decides policy. Refactored zrbfc_wait_build_completion: per-poll-attempt files numbered by z_polls (already a loop counter, no new variable per BCG Exception 2); failure-branch warnings now surface 'rc=${z_curl_rc}' and 'see ${z_stderr_file}' (mirrors rbgv:140 wording exactly); added buc_log_pipe of stderr file content into transcript; tracks z_last_good_response and copies to ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE at loop exit (rbgu's 'register winner under bare infix' pattern at rbgu:357-359). External consumers of ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1040,1264 unaffected — bare-name contract preserved. Removed unused ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE kindle constant (no external consumers). Tolerance at z_max_consecutive_failures=3 unchanged — Pass-1 collects rc data; if next empirical failure surfaces a real >60s outage, Pass-2 calibrates tolerance on observed rc distribution rather than guessing. Behavior delta on rbfc poll: now sends 'Accept: application/json' header it previously omitted (Cloud Build REST API returns JSON regardless of Accept; benign canonicalization). Verification: bash -n green on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA3/BBAA4 baseline); shellcheck warnings on both files are all pre-existing (kindle vars consumed cross-module, $(<file) on local declarations) — no new warnings from this notch.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
33798bfc  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight shebang for alpine: switch from #!/bin/bash to #!/bin/sh in both the source script (rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh:1) and the preflight step assembly (zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:551, which strips line 1 of the source and re-prepends a shebang). Empirical onboarding-sequence run after the substitution-syntax fix surfaced a new Cloud Build failure: step 0 'reliquary-preflight' on the alpine reliquary image exited 255 with 'exec script: no such file or directory' — the classic missing-interpreter ENOENT, here for /bin/bash since alpine ships busybox sh only and bash is not installed. Convention check across the 14 step scripts in rbgj* tree (rbgja*, rbgjb*, rbgje*, rbgji*, rbgjm*, rbgjr*, rbgjv*) showed an established precedent: 13 of 14 scripts use #!/bin/bash and run on docker/gcloud images that ship bash; the one outlier rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh uses #!/bin/sh and runs on the alpine reliquary tool image — wired via the explicit shell column 'sh' in the rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:469 step-defs table. The new preflight broke the pattern by advertising bash while running on alpine. Edit #2 (the assembly's printf) is the load-bearing one — the assembly's tail -n +2 strips the source shebang anyway, so the source line never reaches Cloud Build. Edit #1 (the source shebang) keeps the file consistent with the rbgjv01 precedent that source shebang matches runtime shell. Script body is already POSIX-compatible: test, case, for, ${VAR%:*}, ${VAR##*:}, printf, grep/cut, [ -z ], >&2; the only mild non-POSIX is set -euo pipefail which busybox ash has supported since 1.27 (2017) and modern alpine ships >=1.36. Verification: bash -n green on both edited files; full re-run pending after operator restarts the chat in clean pace remount.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
9ea3b9fa  ₢BBAAs    fix in-pool reliquary preflight script: drop bash default-value syntax from the three enshrine-locator reads so Cloud Build's automapSubstitutions scanner recognizes them as references. Empirical onboarding-sequence run surfaced HTTP 400 'key _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n in the substitution data is not matched in the template' on the very first ordain (conjure of rbev-sentry-deb-tether). Direct inspection of the rejected build payload (rbfd_context_build.json) showed the four GAR/RELIQUARY substitutions referenced as ${_KEY} bare in the script body — accepted by Cloud Build — and the three ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n substitutions referenced as ${_KEY:-} (bash default-value form) — rejected. Only structural difference between matched and unmatched. The runtime guard one line below (test -n LOCATOR || continue) already handles the empty-string case for non-conjure modes, so the :- defaults were doing no semantic work. This was the BBAA3 wrap-criterion run that dca68895 explicitly deferred to BBAAs; the bug was guaranteed to surface on first empirical submission. Verification: bash -n green; full re-run pending after operator restarts the chat.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
a230c845  ₢BBAAs    coronet-in-source sweep on rbtdro_onboarding.rs commit-message strings: per operator directive 'coronets should never be installed into source', remove the BBAA0/BBAAu prefixes from the nine commit-message string literals passed to rbtdro_git_commit. The strings are runtime-built commit subjects — 'BBAAu kludge-{}: sentry hallmark', 'BBAAu inscribe-reliquary: ...', 'BBAA0 ordain-airgap: ...', etc. — which embedded the coronet identifier of the pace that originally landed each function as a static string literal in source. Per directive that's an installation of coronets in source. Sites: line 565 kludge-sentry, 590 kludge-bottle, 648 inscribe-reliquary, 739 ordain-conjure, 787 conjure-srjcl, 826 ordain-airgap-step1, 896 ordain-airgap-step3, 946 ordain-bind, 992 ordain-graft. After this sweep the commit subject reads from action ('kludge-tadmor: sentry hallmark') without coronet prefix. Out of scope for this notch and deferred as broader follow-up: docstring/comment refs to past paces in rbtdrc_crucible.rs (lines 92/196/2123 reference '₢BBAAt'), rbtdtk_canonical.rs:56 (BBAAd policy gate), rbte_cli.sh:68 (BBAAS pattern); error-string coronets in rbtdrp_pristine.rs lines 958/983 ('(BBAAN divest-deletes-production-RBRA contract violated)') which become user-facing diagnostics; spec refs in RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc:41 and RBSDR-director_roster.adoc:39 ('per BBAAQ contract'). Those are different shape (historical-context refs vs commit-string templates) and warrant operator review of whether the directive applies symmetrically. Verification: theurge build green; bash -n n/a (Rust file); 9 occurrences of BBAA prefix grepped from this file before edits, 0 after. 805d08d6  ₢BBAAs    spec/comment drift cleanup after enshrine simplification: align RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc, RBS0-SpecTop.adoc rbrv_image_origin/rbrv_image_anchor linked-term descriptions, rbhoda_director_airgap handbook prose, and the airgap-bottle rbrv.env head comment with the simplified bash. RBSAE: replace 'skip-if-set semantics + Director HEAD-validates' head NOTE with 'pure mirror — every call inspects current upstream, computes the locator from live manifest digest, copies to GAR, overwrites RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR'; add adjacent NOTE that hallmark-pin slots (airgap-bottle) do not invoke this operation, with hallmark existence established by producer's ordain success; Validate Input requires ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape since ANCHOR is a derived value); Classify Slots step removed entirely; Submit step header drops 'only if any slot is to-enshrine' qualifier (always submitted); Extract step drops 'only for to-enshrine slots' qualifier and 'pre-populated slots not touched' clause, replaced with 'overwriting any prior value'. RBS0 [[rbrv_image_origin]]: replace open-vs-pre-populated framing with upstream-base-vs-hallmark-base — upstream-base says enshrine overwrites ANCHOR every call, hallmark-base says enshrine is not invoked. RBS0 [[rbrv_image_anchor]]: same simplification on the namespace-keyed bullets — rbi_es written/overwritten by enshrine as derived value of current upstream, rbi_hm written by orchestration with no enshrine invocation. rbhoda_director_airgap step 1 prose: drop 'slots whose ANCHOR is already populated are HEAD-validated and left untouched' framing, replace with 'inspects current upstream, computes locator from live manifest digest, overwrites RBRV ANCHOR — re-running mirrors current upstream and surfaces upstream rotation as a different locator'. Step 2 prose: drop 'run enshrine on the airgap vessel as a validation step' paragraph entirely, replace with 'enshrine is not invoked on the airgap vessel — the hallmark's existence in your DEPOT is established by the forge's ordain success above; conjure resolves the locator at airgap-bottle build time'. Step 'pattern' summary: link 2 reworded from 're-enshrine its hallmark' to 'write its hallmark locator into the consumer's RBRV ANCHOR'. rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env head comment: drop 'Enshrine then HEAD-validates it (skip-if-set)', replace with 'Conjure resolves it at airgap-bottle build time; enshrine is not invoked on this vessel'. Other touch-checks: zrbfd_registry_preflight HEAD check (rbfd:341) is the ordain-side preflight and stays untouched per design — different purpose (fast-fail before launching conjure CB if enshrined image missing); RCG/AXLA/APCK 'classifier' references are unrelated naming-convention or PHI-clipboard contexts. BCG audit on edited bash file (rbhoda_director_airgap.sh): no test-vs-bracket changes, no new variable patterns, only buh_line / buh_code / buh_e prose changes; bash -n green. AsciiDoc edits preserve linked-term attribute references and structure (NOTE blocks, step bullets). Verification: bash -n green on rbhoda_director_airgap.sh and rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap/rbrv.env.
